subject: GRB 141102A: Fermi GBM detection
body:
Bin-Bin Zhang (UAH) reports on behalf of the Fermi GBM Team:

"At 12:51:39.26 UT on the 2nd of November 2014, the Fermi Gamma-Ray Burst Monitor
ïŋ―ïŋ―triggered and located GRB 141102A (trigger 436625502 / 141102536), which was alsoïŋ―ïŋ―
detected by Swift (Cummings 2014, GCN 17020). The GBM on-ground location, usingïŋ―ïŋ―
the Fermi GBM trigger data, is consistent with the Swift location. The trigger resulted in
ïŋ―ïŋ―an Autonomous Repoint Request (ARR) that was accepted and the spacecraft slewed
ïŋ―ïŋ―to the GBM in-flight location. The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is about 57 degrees.

The GBM light curve consists of two main peaks with a total duration (T90) of about
ïŋ―ïŋ―2.62 s (50ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―300 keV). The time-averaged spectrum from T0ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―0.064 s to T0+2.56 s isïŋ―ïŋ―
well fit by a power law function with an exponential high-energy cutoff. The power lawïŋ―ïŋ―
index is ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―0.85 +/- 0.12 and the cutoff energy, parameterized as Epeak, is 770 +/- 243 keV.

The event fluence (10ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―1000 keV) in this time interval is (1.7 +/- 0.1)Eïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―06 erg/cm^2. The
1-sec peak photon flux measured starting from T0+1.1 s in the 10ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―1000 keV band isïŋ―ïŋ―
5.0 +/- 0.2 ph/s/cm2.

The spectral analysis results presented above are preliminary; final results will be published
ïŋ―ïŋ―in the GBM GRB Catalog."
